question: What are the key factors to check when troubleshooting technical issues or malfunctions?
content:
Use the troubleshooting checklist by matching the symptom to the marked checks. For no power, no picture or sound, poor picture, poor reception, lines, ghosts, remote-control failure, or inability to tune a channel, check whether the power cord is plugged in, whether the television is turned on, the remote-control batteries, another channel to rule out station trouble, the antenna connections, local interference, antenna position, picture control settings, and whether the channel has been programmed with AUTO PROGRAM.<PIC>
If the checklist does not resolve the problem or the TV needs service, do not remove the cover or back; refer servicing to qualified personnel.
ret: "Use the troubleshooting checklist by matching the symptom to the marked checks. For no power, no picture or sound, poor picture, poor reception, lines, ghosts, remote-control failure, or inability to tune a channel, check whether the power cord is plugged in, whether the television is turned on, the remote-control batteries, another channel to rule out station trouble, the antenna connections, local interference, antenna position, picture control settings, and whether the channel has been programmed with AUTO PROGRAM.<PIC>\nIf the checklist does not resolve the problem or the TV needs service, do not remove the cover or back; refer servicing to qualified personnel.",["Manual35_45"]

evidence_refs.note: Troubleshooting checklist heading and embedded checklist image.
Safety/service cautions state not to remove the cover or service the television yourself.

Checklist matrix lists symptoms and the checks: power cord, TV power, remote batteries, another channel, antenna connections, local interference, antenna adjustment, picture control, and AUTO PROGRAM.

notes: Answered from the Color Television troubleshooting checklist. One image is used because the checklist items and symptom-to-check matrix are contained in the embedded figure.
